name: Unicorn Engine (CPU Emulator Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0); Nguyen Anh Quynh (same as Capstone author)

performance.notes: QEMU-derived emulation; fast for instruction-level emulation

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-architecture CPU emulation. from unicorn import *. from unicorn.x86_const import *. mu = Uc(UC_ARCH_X86, UC_MODE_64). mu.mem_map(ADDRESS, 2*1024*1024). mu.mem_write(ADDRESS, X86_CODE). mu.reg_write(UC_X86_REG_RIP, ADDRESS). mu.emu_start(ADDRESS, ADDRESS + len(X86_CODE)). Hooks: mu.hook_add(UC_HOOK_CODE, callback). Architectures: x86/64, ARM, ARM64, MIPS, SPARC, M68K. Used in angr, malware analysis, shellcode testing. QEMU-derived engine.

gotchas: Emulation-only — no syscall handling (combine with angr or custom hooks)
GPL-2.0 license may affect commercial tool distribution
Complex setup for realistic OS emulation
Memory mapping and initialization requires deep binary knowledge
best_when: Shellcode testing without executing on real CPU
When symbolic execution (angr) is overkill for pure emulation
avoid_when: When angr's symbolic execution provides better analysis
Simple shellcode testing (pwntools process() may suffice)
summary: Unicorn Engine is a multi-architecture CPU emulator. Uc(UC_ARCH_X86, UC_MODE_64).mem_map().mem_write().emu_start() emulates x86/ARM/MIPS/SPARC/M68K code. Hooks for instruction callbacks. QEMU-derived. Used for malware deobfuscation, shellcode testing, and research. No syscall support — combine with custom hooks or angr. GPL-2.0 license. Lower-level than angr but faster for pure emulation tasks.
